content_of_post_to_comment:
i've hit a wall in productivity for the past couple of years and its really getting to me. I want a completely different life and it seems my methods of learning how to improve are failing me. I seem to be great at adopting new theories and mastering them but never implementing them to scale my goals. my methods (use a calendar, pomodoro studying, notion habit tracker, to do list making sure theres only 3 actionable items that seriously push the needle so im not wasting time, a series of learning, implementing, reflecting then iterating, building an achievement list of things ive achieved to make note of my progress, tracking my time during the day to track what it is im actually doing with my time, then finally just pushing myself to lock in and work harder) were great for when i was just getting into taking initative for myself and developing myself into who i want to be. however, now that i've established skills, plans, and routines, the actual carrying out and evolution of said plans continue to fail. I will reach a new height, make progress then completely flatline into months of stagnation then eventually decline until i get fed up and decide to will power my way to new heights. however no continuity, it's so fucking frustrating i want to win.

to be less vague im currently building a trading business. where i develop my own investment thesis and accross different timeframes utilize my economics and financial knowledge in conjunction with quantitative strategies like math, statistics and programming to profit from the financial markets. more plainly, day trading i guess. of that i know the internet is filled with gurus telling you youll make 6 figures doing fuckall behind your laptop with 3 cents in your bank account. thankfully ive wanted to go into finance ever since i watched the wolf of wallstreet at 14(im 19 now) and have been studying and built a small cute bookkeeping firm with a few clients but im just so ready for the next level. anyways point is, how i get this new life for myself involves lots of academics. reading, writing, analyzing, critiquing, etc. however i seem to not be able to consistently to do the work it takes for me to make meaningful progress (work more than 1-2hrs/day consistently out the week) within each year (as you can tell im reflecting on the past 6months). I will say i did start going to the library and i would do 4-hrs/day 4 days a week cz they close early on fridays. that was great, i stopped because i felt so uncomfortable i have horrible self esteem issues at times, taking the bus there, eating my food from home because im too poor to buy food, seeing the same people everyday i just felt so insecure i was like man what an idiot you are you've been coming here for 3 months now, are you profitable yet? and its like that's such a ridiculous question i have sooo much to study for, 3 months isnt enough time to assess my success. but going there everyday and seeing people made me feel compelled to turn up with something.

anyways im a lost kid for sure. i know i have the academic prowess to take this on but idk these past couple years as i grow older and having big dreams and goals in and of themselves arent much posistive reinforcement than the actualization of said ambition and it has me struggling with the practical aspect of making my dreams come to life. i know i cant be the only one whos been through this. i also have lots of insomnia because theres such a massive soul crushing level of guilt i feel every night and my desire to ascend intensifies when the world goes quiet. im in therapy and for the most part my anxiety and depression are things i can manage but can i even say that if im not making progress in my productive goals?

TLDR: i am great at adopting a theory or idea, lets say i struggle with being organized throughout the day or getting everything done. so then the solution is use a calendar but then i dont use my calendar all the time and it becomes irregular yet i still understand the calendar is the best way to organize my life but it doesnt help me get more shit done than i was before. why can i not see through reaching new scale after i've implemented new ideas/solutions to my problems productivity wise?

idk what to do anymore, please help.
comment: I think you might be running into the gap between building systems and building the identity of someone who shows up when the excitement is gone. The library story stood out because you were measuring yourself against being profitable after 3 months instead of measuring the reps. Trading especially has a long boring phase where the work feels like it’s going nowhere. Maybe the goal is to make the process boring enough that your mood doesn’t get a vote.
